decisions.title: Approve Artifacts RFC for Canvas evolution
decisions.context: ENG-10663 proposes artifacts system - critical for Canvas V2 and 'Build the Moat' pillar. Must decide on scope and implementation approach to maintain Q2 momentum.

arguments.for: Enables richer Canvas experiences, supports Python Rules execution context, differentiates from competitors
arguments.against: May introduce complexity that delays core Canvas features, requires infrastructure investment

decisions.title: Commit to multi-model strategy vs. single provider
decisions.context: ENG-10351, 10383-84, 10352 show active model evaluation across GPT-5.1, Gemma 31B, others. Ask SIFT quality depends on model selection. Need architectural decision.

arguments.for: Model diversity improves resilience, allows task-specific optimization, reduces vendor lock-in
arguments.against: Increases operational complexity, makes quality guarantees harder, fragments evaluation efforts

decisions.title: Prioritize conversation UX improvements for Ask SIFT GA
decisions.context: ENG-10722, 10311, 10430, 10392, 10385 show heavy investment in conversation features. Must decide if these are GA-blockers or post-launch enhancements.
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear GA success criteria for Ask SIFT - need alignment on 'Nail the Core' definition

arguments.for: User file uploads and robust state machine are table stakes for production AI assistants
arguments.against: Delays GA launch which impacts North Star metric (decisions captured), can iterate post-launch

decisions.title: Approve internal skills system architecture
decisions.context: ENG-10554 proposes internally-built skills system - foundational for 'Embed Deeper' by allowing custom decision workflows. Build vs. integrate decision needed.

arguments.for: Custom-built gives full control over decision capture tracking, tighter integration with Explore/Canvas
arguments.against: Third-party tools accelerate time-to-market, reduce maintenance burden, proven patterns

decisions.title: Allocate resources to FedRAMP migration vs. new features
decisions.context: ENG-10702, 10720, 9694 indicate active FedRAMP/GovCloud work. Competes with feature development for Sift Edge and other Q2 commitments.

arguments.for: FedRAMP unlocks enterprise deals, supports 'Build the Moat' through compliance differentiation
arguments.against: Diverts engineering from customer-facing features that drive North Star metric adoption

timeAudit.weekSummary: 75% meeting load exceeds healthy threshold - recommend async updates for status meetings
alerts: Zero focus blocks scheduled Friday - strategic planning time at risk
3 overlapping 1:1s with Ask SIFT team indicate coordination bottleneck
FedRAMP sync meetings not tied to decision deadlines
